DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-26-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Tyler B. Saldo of Saldo Law Group (805-547-00700) appears as counsel for plaintiff/responding party

Attorney, Jessica Munoz of Carroll Kelly Trotter Franzen et al (562-432-5855) appears telephonically as counsel for defendant/moving party, Dignity Health

Attorney, Bethany Peak of LeBeau-Thelen LLP (661-325-8962) appears telephonically as counsel for defendant/non-opposing party, Imad Abumeri, MD

Attorney, Stephanie L. Roundy of Riggio Mordaunt & Kelly (209-473-8732) appears telephonically as counsel for defendant/non-opposing party, Bakersfield Neuroscience and Spine Institute

The Court issues a tentative ruling to overrule the demurrer. The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH DBA MERCY HOSPITALS OF BAKERSFIELD's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. Defendant cites no authority holding that a hospital's staff cannot be held liable for medical battery. Likewise, defendant cites no authority barring "duplicative" causes of action in two lawsuits; the cases defendant cites regard duplicative causes of action within one suit. Prevailing party to submit a [proposed] written order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Laura Reding, CSR 711, email: lreding@sftc.org. (D302)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-22-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Tyler Saldo of Saldo Law Group (805-547-0700) appears telephonically as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Stephanie Roundy of Riggio Mordaunt & Kelly (209-473-8732) appears telephonically as counsel for defendant/demurring party, Bakersfield Neuroscience and Spine Institute

Attorney, Chelsie Morgan of LeBeau-Thelen LLP (661-325-8962) appears telephonically as counsel for defendant, Imad Abumeri M.D.

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT BAKERSFIELD NEUROSCIENCE & SPINE INSTITUTE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. Plaintiff adequately pleads a claim for medical battery. "When the patient gives permission to perform one type of treatment and the doctor performs another, the requisite element of deliberate intent to deviate from the consent given is present." (Cobbs v. Grant (1972) 8 Cal.3d 229, 240; CACI 530A.) And "whether operating on the wrong disk within inches of the correct disk is a 'substantially different procedure''is a factual question for the finder of fact to decide'not one capable of being decided on demurrer." (Kaplan v. Mamelak (2008) 162 Cal.App.4th 637,647.) The cases cited on the "primary right theory" are inapposite. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, bus. tel: (530) 339-5023, email: rhunter@sftc.org. (D302)
